# Railway env model

Per-service variables + shared values referenced into each service. Three
mechanisms, never mixed:

1. **Shared vars** — truly-external secrets/endpoints used by >1 service or
   that must match across services/Phala. Defined **once** in
   `_shared.env`, set as Railway **project shared variables**, consumed via
   `${{shared.NAME}}`. Source of truth: `_shared.env` (gitignored).
2. **Reference vars** — internal routing between Railway services
   (managed-PG `DATABASE_URL`, sibling-service URLs). **Never** put these in
   an env file — `bootstrap.sh` wires them as
   `${{<svc>.RAILWAY_PRIVATE_DOMAIN}}` / `${{<db>.DATABASE_URL}}`.
3. **Service-local vars** — a service's own config + any service-specific
   secret. Lives in `railway/env/<service>.env` (gitignored).

`*.env` here is gitignored. `*.example` + this README are tracked. Copy
each `<svc>.env.example` → `<svc>.env` and fill, then `bootstrap.sh`
applies: shared → project shared vars; `<svc>.env` → that service; the
reference vars it wires automatically.

## Shared vars (`_shared.env`)

| Var | Origin | Used by |
|---|---|---|
| `OBS_INGEST_TOKEN` | generated (✅ exists in .env.phala.prod) | alloy-v3, Phala sidecar |
| `ROUTER_DETECTOR_API_KEY` | from .env.phala.prod | sauron, Phala |
| `ROUTER_ADMIN_API_KEY` | from .env.phala.prod | admin-dashboard, Phala |
| `ROUTER_GATEWAY_API_KEY` | from .env.phala.prod | router-gateway, Phala |
| `TOKEN_INDEXER_API_KEY` | **generate** (== each indexer's `EVM_TOKEN_INDEXER_API_KEY`) | sauron, evm-token-indexers |
| `HL_SHIM_API_KEY` | **generate** | sauron, hl-shim-indexer |
| `POSTGRES_REPLICA_PASSWORD` | from `.env.phala.prod` | router-physical-standby |
| `POSTGRES_REPLICA_PASSWORD_URLENCODED` | derived by `bootstrap.sh` from `POSTGRES_REPLICA_PASSWORD` | router-physical-standby `DATABASE_URL` |
| `ETH_RPC_URL` `BASE_RPC_URL` `ARBITRUM_RPC_URL` | from .env.phala.prod | sauron, receipt-watchers, ponder (HTTP) |
| `ETH_WS_RPC_URL` `BASE_WS_RPC_URL` `ARBITRUM_WS_RPC_URL` | **PROCURE** (none on hand) | receipt-watchers, ponder |
| `BITCOIN_RPC_URL` | managed rathole broker v3 | sauron, bitcoin-indexer/-receipt-watcher |
| `BITCOIN_RPC_AUTH` | from .env.phala.prod | sauron, bitcoin-indexer/-receipt-watcher |
| `BITCOIN_ZMQ_RAWBLOCK/RAWTX/SEQUENCE_ENDPOINT` | managed rathole broker v3 | sauron, bitcoin-indexer/-receipt-watcher |
| `ELECTRUM_HTTP_SERVER_URL` | from .env.phala.prod | sauron, bitcoin-indexer |
| `HYPERUNIT_API_URL` | fixed external endpoint (`https://api.hyperunit.xyz`) | sauron |
| `ROUTER_CDC_PUBLICATION_NAME` `ROUTER_CDC_MESSAGE_PREFIX` | fixed convention | sauron, admin-dashboard |
| `ROUTER_INTERNAL_BASE_URL` | **deploy-time** (Phala router-api public URL) | sauron, router-gateway |

## Reference vars (auto — `bootstrap.sh`, NOT in any env file)

`SAURON_STATE_DATABASE_URL`, `HL_SHIM_DATABASE_URL`,
`ROUTER_GATEWAY_DATABASE_URL`, token-indexer `DATABASE_URL`,
`ADMIN_DASHBOARD_{AUTH,ANALYTICS}_DATABASE_URL`,
`router-physical-standby-v3.DATABASE_URL`,
`(ADMIN_DASHBOARD_)?ROUTER_REPLICA_DATABASE_URL`, `HYPERUNIT_PROXY_URL`, and
every inter-service `*_INDEXER_URL` / `*_RECEIPT_WATCHER_URL` /
`HL_SHIM_INDEXER_URL`.

## Per-service files

Each `<service>.env` holds ONLY that service's service-local vars. The
shared refs + reference vars it also receives are listed in its
`.example` header for clarity but are applied by `bootstrap.sh`, not the
file. Services: `sauron`, `hl-shim-indexer`,
`evm-receipt-watcher-{ethereum,base,arbitrum}`, `bitcoin-indexer`,
`bitcoin-receipt-watcher`, `evm-token-indexer-{ethereum,base,arbitrum}`,
`router-gateway`, `admin-dashboard`, `explorer`,
`router-physical-standby`, `router-replica-stunnel`,
`upstream-socks5-proxy`, `hyperunit-socks5-proxy`,
`sauron-bitcoin-rathole-broker`.

## Procurement status (truly-external, cannot generate)

- 🔴 `*_WS_RPC_URL` (eth/base/arb) — WS-capable provider endpoints. **Not on hand.**
- ⏳ `ROUTER_INTERNAL_BASE_URL` (post-Phala), `ALLOY_V3_OTLP_URL` (post-Railway) — produced during deploy.
- 🟡 Generated by tooling: `TOKEN_INDEXER_API_KEY`, `HL_SHIM_API_KEY`,
  `ROUTER_GATEWAY_CANCELLATION_SECRET_KEY`, `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET`.
